responseMessageContents.text:
Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me create the edit equipment screen. I need to:

1. Create `expo/app/edit-equipment.tsx` - the edit form screen
2. Register it in `expo/app/_layout.tsx`
3. Update the edit button in `expo/app/my-equipment.tsx` to navigate to the edit screen
4. Add necessary translation keys for the edit flow

Let me build this now.

responseMessageContents.text:
All done. Here's the summary:

**Files changed:**

1. **`expo/app/edit-equipment.tsx`** — New file. Full edit form screen for equipment listings.
2. **`expo/app/_layout.tsx`** — Registered the `edit-equipment` route in the stack navigator.
3. **`expo/app/my-equipment.tsx`** — Updated the Edit button to navigate to `/edit-equipment?id=...` instead of the details page.
4. **`expo/i18n/translations.ts`** — Added translation keys for both AR/EN: `edit_listing`, `save_changes`, `saving_changes`, `update_success`, `update_failed`, `load_failed`, `existing_images`, `new_images`.

**What's working now:**

- **Edit button** in My Equipment now opens a real editable form pre-filled with the existing equipment data
- **Existing images** are displayed and can be individually removed
- **New images** can be added via the image picker (same Cloudinary upload flow as create)
- **On save**, `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` is called, which:
  - Compares old images vs final images
  - Deletes removed Cloudinary images via the Cloudflare Worker
  - Updates the Firestore document
- **Backward compatibility** preserved — legacy string image entries and object-based entries both work
- **Owner check** — only the equipment owner can access the edit form
- **Error handling** — upload/save failures show alerts without crashing; partial Cloudinary deletion failures are handled gracefully

**No remaining limitations** for this phase. The delete flow in `my-equipment.tsx` remains untouched and still uses `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup()`.

responseMessageContents.title: Add an edit screen for equipment listings and improve image management.
responseMessageContents.description: You can now edit your equipment listings. We've added a new screen that lets you change the title, description, category, location, and price of your items. You can also add new photos or remove existing ones. When you remove a photo, it will now be correctly deleted from our storage to keep things clean. Only the owner of a listing can make these changes.
